title: Celtis leveillei
intro: Celtis leveillei, also known as the Himalayan hackberry or Chinese hackberry, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its purported health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties that may help to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as aid in wound healing and improve digestion. The bioactive constituents of Celtis leveillei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or an extract, which is often used to treat coughs, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders.
